concepts.title: Sets and functions
concepts.blurb: Sets, membership, subsets, unions/intersections, functions as single-valued relations, injections and surjections.

concepts.title: Cartesian products and power sets
concepts.blurb: Products $A\times B$, function spaces $B^A$, and the power set $\mathcal{P}(A)\cong\{0,1\}^A$ — the basic constructions behind tuples, graphs of functions, and indicator functions.

concepts.title: Equivalence relations and quotients
concepts.blurb: Equivalence relations partition sets; the quotient set X/∼ as the universal recipient of functions constant on classes.

concepts.title: Countability
concepts.blurb: Countable vs uncountable, Cantor's diagonal argument, and why |R| > |Q|.

concepts.title: Axiom of choice
concepts.blurb: A choice function exists for every family of non-empty sets. Equivalent to Zorn's lemma and the well-ordering theorem; behind every basis, maximal ideal, and Tychonoff product.
